workflow_rationale: This is a straightforward documentation task that creates setup instructions for an Android development environment. No code implementation, refactoring, or multi-service integration required. The deliverable is a single comprehensive documentation file.

phases.name: Research Android Development Requirements
phases.description: Gather information about current Android development best practices, SDK versions, tool requirements, and common setup patterns

subtasks.description: Research current Android SDK requirements and recommended versions
subtasks.expected_output: Document with Android SDK version requirements (API levels, build tools), Java/Kotlin version compatibility, and Gradle version recommendations

verification.instructions: Review research_notes.md to ensure it contains: (1) Android SDK API levels, (2) Java/Kotlin versions, (3) Gradle versions, (4) Android Studio requirements

subtasks.notes: Research completed successfully. Created comprehensive research_notes.md with: (1) Android SDK API levels including current requirements (API 35 for Android 15), (2) Java 17 and Kotlin 2.3.20+ version requirements, (3) Gradle 9.4.1 and AGP 9.1.0 versions, (4) Android Studio Panda 2 (2025.3.2) requirements including 16GB RAM recommendation and system requirements. All required sections documented with sources.

subtasks.description: Document IDE setup requirements and configuration
subtasks.expected_output: Documentation of Android Studio installation process, SDK Manager usage, AVD Manager configuration, and essential plugins

verification.instructions: Verify research notes include Android Studio setup steps, SDK Manager usage, and emulator configuration

subtasks.notes: Added comprehensive IDE setup documentation including: Android Studio installation steps for Windows/macOS/Linux, detailed SDK Manager setup and usage guide, complete emulator configuration with AVD creation, hardware acceleration setup for all platforms, performance optimization tips, troubleshooting guide, and IDE configuration recommendations. Documentation includes code examples, command-line options, and best practices for 2026 development environment.

subtasks.description: Identify common setup issues and troubleshooting solutions
subtasks.expected_output: List of common Android development environment issues (PATH problems, SDK license acceptance, emulator performance, build errors) with solutions

verification.instructions: Verify research notes include at least 5 common troubleshooting scenarios with solutions

subtasks.notes: Added comprehensive troubleshooting section with 8 common Android development setup issues covering environment variables, SDK licenses, Gradle compatibility, emulator acceleration, memory optimization, ADB connections, network/proxy issues, and missing SDK components. Each scenario includes symptoms, root causes, detailed solutions, verification steps, and prevention tips. Exceeds requirement of 5 scenarios.

phases.name: Create Setup Documentation
phases.description: Write comprehensive setup guide covering prerequisites, installation, IDE configuration, project initialization, and troubleshooting

subtasks.description: Create main SETUP.md file with prerequisites section

verification.instructions: Verify SETUP.md exists and contains: (1) Table of contents, (2) Prerequisites section with OS requirements, (3) Software requirements list

subtasks.notes: Successfully created SETUP.md with comprehensive prerequisites section. Includes: (1) Complete table of contents with 7 main sections and subsections, (2) Operating System requirements for Windows 10/11, macOS 11.0+, and Ubuntu 20.04+ LTS with architecture notes, (3) Detailed hardware requirements covering minimum (8GB RAM, 20GB disk), recommended (16GB RAM, 50GB SSD), and optimal (32GB RAM, 100GB+ SSD) configurations, (4) Complete software requirements list including Android Studio Panda 2 (2025.3.2), JDK 17, Android SDK API 35, Kotlin 2.3.20+, Gradle 9.4.1, AGP 9.1.0, Git, and hardware acceleration tools. File structure includes placeholders for future sections to be completed in subsequent subtasks.

subtasks.description: Document Android SDK and tools installation

verification.instructions: Verify SETUP.md includes step-by-step Android SDK installation for Windows, macOS, and Linux with command-line verification steps

subtasks.notes: Documented Android SDK installation with step-by-step instructions for Windows, macOS, and Linux. Included environment variable configuration, command-line verification steps, GUI and CLI installation methods, required SDK components, and troubleshooting section. All verification requirements met.

subtasks.description: Document Android Studio IDE setup and configuration

verification.instructions: Verify SETUP.md includes: (1) Android Studio download/install, (2) SDK Manager setup, (3) AVD creation, (4) Essential plugins list

subtasks.notes: Android Studio IDE setup documentation completed. Added comprehensive sections covering: (1) SDK Manager configuration with step-by-step installation instructions, (2) AVD creation guide with device profiles and troubleshooting, (3) Essential plugins list with 18+ categorized recommendations, (4) IDE configuration and best practices. All verification criteria met.

verification.instructions: Verify SETUP.md includes steps to: (1) Create new Android project, (2) Configure build.gradle, (3) Set up project structure, (4) Initialize Git repository

subtasks.notes: Project Initialization section successfully added to SETUP.md. Documented: (1) Creating new Android project with template selection and project configuration including package naming, SDK versions (minSdk 24, targetSdk 35), and language selection, (2) Complete project structure explanation with Android view and key files (AndroidManifest.xml, MainActivity.kt, build.gradle.kts), (3) Detailed build.gradle configuration covering project-level and module-level build files, gradle.properties optimization, and version catalog (libs.versions.toml), (4) Project structure organization with package-by-feature vs package-by-layer approaches and resource naming conventions, (5) Comprehensive Git initialization guide with configuration, .gitignore setup, initial commit, branching strategy, and remote repository connection, (6) Troubleshooting section for common project initialization issues (Gradle sync, SDK location, Git configuration). All verification requirements met.

verification.instructions: Verify SETUP.md includes: (1) Gradle build commands, (2) Running on emulator, (3) Running on physical device, (4) Debug vs release builds

subtasks.notes: Added comprehensive build and run instructions to SETUP.md including: Gradle build commands (assembleDebug, assembleRelease, clean, bundle), running on Android Emulator with detailed AVD setup, running on physical devices with USB debugging configuration, Debug vs Release builds comparison with signing configuration, advanced build options, and CI/CD examples. All verification requirements met: (1) Gradle build commands ✓, (2) Running on emulator ✓, (3) Running on physical device ✓, (4) Debug vs release builds ✓

verification.instructions: Verify SETUP.md includes troubleshooting section with: (1) Common errors, (2) Solutions, (3) Additional resources, (4) Support contact info

subtasks.notes: Added comprehensive troubleshooting section to SETUP.md including: (1) Common errors organized by category (Android Studio, SDK, Gradle, Emulator, Build, Git, ADB, Device Connection, Performance), (2) Multiple actionable solutions for each issue with platform-specific commands, (3) Additional resources section with Stack Overflow, Android Issue Tracker, official docs, and community links, (4) Support contact information with email and diagnostic information collection guide. Total: 715 lines added covering 9 major categories of issues with detailed solutions.

phases.name: Review and Finalize
phases.description: Review documentation for completeness, accuracy, clarity, and proper formatting

subtasks.description: Review documentation for completeness and accuracy

verification.instructions: Verify: (1) All sections are complete, (2) Instructions are clear and sequential, (3) Commands are accurate, (4) Links work, (5) Formatting is consistent, (6) No typos or grammatical errors

subtasks.notes: Documentation review completed successfully. Verified all sections complete, instructions clear and sequential, commands accurate, links functional, formatting consistent, and no grammatical errors. Created comprehensive review summary documenting findings. Document is production-ready.

verification.instructions: Verify README.md exists and includes a clear link/reference to SETUP.md for development environment setup instructions

subtasks.notes: Successfully created README.md with comprehensive project overview and multiple clear references to SETUP.md. The README includes: (1) Prominent "Getting Started" section with direct link to SETUP.md as the first step for new developers, (2) Seven references to SETUP.md throughout the document for different contexts (setup, troubleshooting, contributing, help), (3) Project overview with features and technology stack, (4) Quick start commands for building and running, (5) Documentation section highlighting SETUP.md, (6) Contributing guidelines referencing setup requirements, (7) Help section pointing to SETUP.md troubleshooting. File successfully committed to git. All verification requirements met: README.md exists and includes clear, prominent links to SETUP.md for development environment setup instructions.

deliverables: SETUP.md - Comprehensive development environment setup guide
README.md - Project overview with link to setup guide
research_notes.md - Supporting research documentation (in spec directory)

parallelism.notes: Documentation tasks are sequential by nature. Research must complete before writing, and writing must complete before review.

acceptance_criteria: SETUP.md file exists and is comprehensive
Installation steps are clear and OS-specific
Build and run commands are provided

verification_steps.name: Documentation Completeness Check
verification_steps.command: test -f SETUP.md && test -f README.md && echo 'Documentation files exist'

verification_steps.name: Content Review
verification_steps.command: grep -q 'Prerequisites\|Installation\|Android Studio\|Troubleshooting' SETUP.md && echo 'Key sections present' || echo 'Missing sections'

verification_strategy.reasoning: This is a documentation-only task with trivial risk. No functional code is being modified. Validation is primarily manual review of documentation quality and completeness. Automated testing is not applicable for documentation content.

checks: All major sections are present (Prerequisites, Installation, IDE Setup, Project Init, Build/Run, Troubleshooting)

qa_signoff.verdict: APPROVED - Production-ready documentation. All subtasks completed, all acceptance criteria passed, zero issues found.

recoveryNote: Task recovered from stuck state at 2026-03-25T09:00:40.811Z
